title: Fig. 1 in Vector competence of CUlex QUInQUefaSCIaTUS from Santiago Island, Cape Verde, to West Nile Virus: exploring the potential effect of the vector native WolbaCHIa
author: Moura, Aires Januário Fernandes da
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 1 Wolbachia detection in Culex quinquefasciatus from Santiago Island, Cape Verde, based on the Wolbachia surface protein gene (wsp). a Wolbachia prevalence in F1 and F7 generations of wild-type mosquitoes, as well as in the F7 generation group treated with tetracycline (n = 200 per group), analyzed using conventional and real-time PCR. b Wolbachia density on a linear scale relative to the Cx. quinquefasciatus 18S ribosomal gene, as estimated by quantitative real-time PCR.The difference in Wolbachia density between the F1 and F7 generations was assessed using the Mann–Whitney test. Error bars represent 95% confidence intervals from three biological replicates, each containing DNA from a pool of 5 females
steps_raw: Fig. 1 Wolbachia detection in Culex quinquefasciatus from Santiago Island, Cape Verde, based on the Wolbachia surface protein gene (wsp). a Wolbachia prevalence in F1 and F7 generations of wild-type mosquitoes, as well as in the F7 generation group treated with tetracycline (n = 200 per group), analyzed using conventional and real-time PCR. b Wolbachia density on a linear scale relative to the Cx. quinquefasciatus 18S ribosomal gene, as estimated by quantitative real-time PCR.The difference in Wolbachia density between the F1 and F7 generations was assessed using the Mann–Whitney test. Error bars represent 95% confidence intervals from three biological replicates, each containing DNA from a pool of 5 females
